children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A person who violates shall be assessed a civil penalty in an amount computed under subsection (b).section 5201 of this title
not more than $10,000, if the violation involved the use of force or violence, or the threatened use of force or violence, against the person or property of another person; and
not more than $5,000 for any other violation.

children.heading: Relationship to other penalties
children.label: c Relationship to other penalties
children.text: The penalties established by this section shall be in addition to other criminal or civil penalties that may be levied against the person as a result of an activity in violation of .section 5201 of this title

a written complaint from an officer, employee, or agent of the Forest Service, Bureau of Land Management, National Park Service, United States Fish and Wildlife Service, or other Federal agency that a person violated ; orsection 5201 of this title

a sworn affidavit from an individual and a determination by the Secretary that the statement contains sufficient factual allegations to create a reasonable belief that a violation of has occurred;section 5201 of this title

children.heading: Use of penalty money collected
children.label: e Use of penalty money collected

children.text: deposited into the trust fund established pursuant to the Act entitled “An Act to provide that the United States shall aid the States in wildlife-restoration projects, and for other purposes”, approved () (commonly known as the “Pitman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act”), to support the activities authorized by such Act and undertaken by State wildlife management agencies; orSeptember 2, 193716 U.S.C. 669

children.label: A
children.text: the North American Waterfowl Management Plan signed by the Secretary of the Interior and the Minister of Environment for Canada in May 1986; or

children.label: ii
children.text: on private or State-owned lands when the efforts will also provide a benefit to wildlife management objectives on Federal lands.
